# Venue Templates

## When to Use

- Preparing a manuscript for a specific journal (Nature, Science, PLOS, IEEE, etc.)
- Writing a conference paper (NeurIPS, ICML, CHI, etc.)
- Creating an academic research poster
- Drafting grant proposals (NSF, NIH, DOE, DARPA)
- Checking formatting requirements and page limits

## Quick Reference

### Page Limits

| Venue Type | Typical Limit | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Nature Article | 5 pages | ~3000 words excluding refs |
| Science Report | 5 pages | Figures count toward limit |
| PLOS ONE | No limit | Unlimited length |
| NeurIPS | 8 pages | + unlimited refs/appendix |
| ICML | 8 pages | + unlimited refs/appendix |
| NSF Proposal | 15 pages | Project description only |
| NIH R01 | 12 pages | Research strategy |

### Citation Styles by Venue

| Venue | Style | Format |
|---|---|---|
| Nature | Numbered (superscript) | Nature style |
| Science | Numbered (superscript) | Science style |
| PLOS | Numbered (brackets) | Vancouver |
| Cell Press | Author-year | Cell style |
| ACM | Numbered | ACM style |
| IEEE | Numbered (brackets) | IEEE style |
| APA journals | Author-year | APA 7th |

### Figure Requirements

| Venue | Resolution | Format | Color |
|---|---|---|---|
| Nature | 300+ dpi | TIFF, EPS, PDF | RGB or CMYK |
| Science | 300+ dpi | TIFF, PDF | RGB |
| PLOS | 300-600 dpi | TIFF, EPS | RGB |
| IEEE | 300+ dpi | EPS, PDF | RGB or Grayscale |

## Major Venues by Discipline

### Machine Learning & AI
- NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, CVPR, AAAI

### Computer Science
- ACM CHI, SIGKDD, EMNLP, SIGIR

### Biology & Medicine
- Nature, Cell Press, PLOS, ISMB, RECOMB, NEJM, Lancet, JAMA

### Engineering & Physical Sciences
- IEEE Transactions, ASME, AIAA, Physical Review, ACS

### Interdisciplinary
- PNAS, Nature Communications, Science Advances

## Grant Proposal Agencies

### NSF (National Science Foundation)
- 15-page project description
- Project Summary: Overview, Intellectual Merit, Broader Impacts
- Budget, Biographical sketch (3 pages), Data Management Plan

### NIH (National Institutes of Health)
- R01 (multi-year), R21 (exploratory), K Awards (career development)
- Specific Aims: 1 page (most critical)
- Research Strategy: Significance, Innovation, Approach

### DOE (Department of Energy)
- Office of Science, ARPA-E
- Technology Readiness Level descriptions, Commercialization sections

### DARPA
- BAA responses, Heilmeier Catechism framework
- Technical approach, milestones, transition planning

## Workflow

1. **Identify target venue** — journal, conference, agency
2. **Load template** — article, conference, poster, or grant format
3. **Review requirements** — page limits, fonts, margins, citation style, anonymization
4. **Customize template** — title, authors, affiliations
5. **Validate format** — page count, font sizes, margins, references

## Best Practices

1. **Verify currency** — check template date against latest author guidelines
2. **Check official sources** — many journals provide official LaTeX classes
3. **Preserve structure** — don't remove required sections or packages
4. **Don't override** venue-specific formatting
5. **Check page limits** before final submission
6. **Test figures** for resolution compliance
7. **Review anonymization** for double-blind venues
